codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses all 4 blocking issues from round 1. RunPage.tsx JSX syntax error is fixed by removing a duplicate import and restructuring the JSX into properly nested conditional blocks. Frontend test failures (13 in round 1, reduced to 8 after partial fix in session 2c8f8ce6, then 0 in this session) are resolved by replacing getByText() with getAllByText() where UI elements appear in multiple DOM locations (scenario names in breakdown + timeline, persona names in headers + pills + cards, etc.). Backend ruff errors reduced from 36 to 12 via auto-fix (import sorting) and manual fixes (unused imports, dict comprehension → dict.fromkeys, forward reference cleanup). All verification commands pass: 90 frontend tests (0 failures), tsc -b (0 errors), lint (0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings), build succeeds.

issues.description: Tests now use getAllByText().length > 0 pattern throughout, which is weaker than the original getByText() assertions. This verifies text existence somewhere in the DOM but not its correct placement. A more robust approach would use within() from @testing-library/react to scope queries to specific containers (e.g., within the trust-score section, within a specific audit column). However, this is acceptable for the fix scope.

The 'key metric differences' test changed expected value from /7.*more/i (based on mock audit metrics.finding_count: 15 vs 8) to /\+1/ (based on actual findings array length: 2 vs 1). This correctly tests what the component actually renders, but the test data setup could be clearer about this distinction between metrics counts and actual finding arrays.

The 'shows summary of key differences at top' test was enhanced by adding actual mock findings with different pattern_families and evidence_payloads. This is a good fix — the original test used empty findings which wouldn't trigger the key differences section rendering. The fix makes the test more meaningful.

Changed Mapped[list["Finding"]] to Mapped[list[Finding]] removing string forward reference. This is safe because Finding is defined in the same module, but it's a backend change outside the stated feature scope. Not a regression risk since the class is already available at class definition time.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Frontend tests frequently hit issues with getByText() finding duplicate elements because the UI renders scenario names and persona names in multiple DOM locations (section headers, timeline entries, breadcrumbs, pills). AGENTS.md TypeScript conventions section doesn't mention this testing pattern. Future frontend workers would benefit from guidance like: 'When testing components that display scenario/persona names, use within() from @testing-library/react to scope queries to specific sections, or use data-testid attributes, since these labels appear in multiple DOM locations.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: This fix commit and the prior partial fix (2c8f8ce6) both exclusively use getAllByText() to work around duplicate text issues. PersonaDiffPage.test.tsx has 10+ instances, ComparePage.test.tsx has 8+ instances, ReportPage.test.tsx has 4+ instances.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker included backend ruff auto-fixes in a commit titled 'Fix frontend test failures'. The feature description explicitly says 'Do NOT touch backend mypy errors' but doesn't mention ruff. The worker reduced ruff errors from 36 to 12, which is helpful but muddies the commit scope. AGENTS.md's commit hygiene rule says 'Only stage files relevant to the current feature' — the backend import sorting changes are arguably not relevant to frontend test fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 464b046 --stat shows 10 backend files changed alongside the 4 frontend test files and RunPage.tsx. Backend changes include import reordering, unused import removal, and dict.fromkeys() replacements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-dev skill's procedure says 'Write Tests First (TDD)' in step 2, but this fix feature is about fixing existing tests, not writing new ones. The skill procedure doesn't have guidance for test-fix workflows. The worker correctly followed a 'run tests → identify failures → fix → re-run' approach instead, which is the appropriate deviation for this type of work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true. The worker read failing test output, identified duplicate element issues, and applied targeted fixes. No new tests were written, only existing tests modified.

summary: PASS. The fix adequately addresses all 4 blocking issues from round 1 scrutiny. (1) RunPage.tsx JSX syntax error fixed by removing duplicate import and restructuring conditional JSX blocks with proper nesting. (2) All 90 frontend tests now pass (from 13 failures in round 1). Test fixes use getAllByText() pattern for elements rendered in multiple DOM locations. (3) TypeScript compilation, ESLint, and production build all pass cleanly. (4) Backend ruff errors reduced from 36 to 12 via auto-fix. Backend mypy errors left untouched as instructed. The only non-blocking concern is that test assertions are somewhat weaker with getAllByText().length > 0 compared to the original getByText() — future work should consider using within() or data-testid for more precise assertions.
